serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of martabak telur is 6 large pieces, weighing approximately 200g, as described by the vendor.

nutrient_source.title: Murtabak, meat, fried — Nutrient composition per 100g
nutrient_source.organization: FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asian Food Composition Table (SEAFOODSv1) and IMR Malaysia MyFCD; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central analogous entries for egg-and-meat stuffed pancake
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Martabak telur is the Indonesian variant of murtabak. Nutrient values are adapted from FAO/INFOODS SEA murtabak (meat, fried) entry and cross-referenced with IMR Malaysia MyFCD murtabak daging values. The dish shares the same core composition: wheat-flour wrapper, egg, minced meat, spring onion, pan-fried in oil. Values represent a composite estimate for the Indonesian street-food preparation style.

gi_source.title: Murtabak (meat-filled pancake) — GI analogous estimate based on savoury stuffed wheat-flour pancake; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for martabak telur. GI of 57 is estimated from the closest analogous food: savoury stuffed wheat-flour pancake (murtabak / filled pancake category). The University of Sydney GI Database lists filled savoury pancakes and wheat-based stuffed flatbreads in the medium GI range (GI 50–65). A value of 57 is used as a central estimate. Glycemic load per serving = (57 × 46.0g carbohydrate in 200g serving) / 100 = 26.2.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420mg per 100g; ~840mg per 200g serving)
hypertension.guidance: A single 200g serving of martabak telur provides approximately 840mg sodium, representing about 37% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000mg; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and avoid pairing with other high-sodium condiments such as sambal or soy-based sauces.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 57) and moderate carbohydrate load (~46g per 200g serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Martabak telur has a medium GI of approximately 57 and delivers a glycemic load of about 26 per standard serving, which is above the low-GL threshold of 20; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume a reduced portion (e.g. 2–3 pieces) and pair with non-starchy vegetables to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~4.1g per 100g) and cholesterol (~145mg per 100g) from egg and meat filling, combined with pan-frying in oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: A 200g serving of martabak telur contributes approximately 8.2g saturated fat and 290mg dietary cholesterol, both of which are relevant to LDL-cholesterol management; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to occasional servings and choose preparations using leaner minced meat and less frying oil.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Martabak telur is a popular Indonesian savoury street food consisting of a thin, crispy wheat-flour pancake wrapper that is folded around a filling of beaten eggs, minced beef or chicken, spring onion, leek, and spices including curry powder, salt, and pepper. The pancake is pan-fried in generous amounts of cooking oil on a flat griddle until the exterior is golden and crispy while the interior remains moist and eggy. It is one of the most iconic Indonesian street foods, sold widely from roadside carts and dedicated martabak stalls across Indonesia and in Indonesian diaspora communities in Singapore and Malaysia. The dish is closely related to the Middle Eastern mutabbaq and the Malaysian murtabak, all sharing the same concept of a stuffed fried pancake, though the Indonesian version typically uses a thinner, crispier wrapper and a more egg-forward filling. Martabak telur is typically served with a sweet-sour cucumber pickle (acar), a thin brown dipping sauce made from vinegar, sugar, and chilli, and sometimes sambal. Nutritionally, a 200g serving (6 large pieces) provides approximately 496 kcal, 21g protein, 26.4g fat, and 46g carbohydrate, with a notable sodium content of around 840mg. The medium glycemic index (estimated GI 57) and moderate glycemic load make it a food that individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume in controlled portions. The saturated fat and cholesterol content from eggs and meat are relevant considerations for cardiovascular health. It is generally considered halal when prepared with beef or chicken.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for martabak telur exists in any single approved primary source. Nutrient values are adapted from FAO/INFOODS SEA murtabak (meat, fried) and IMR Malaysia MyFCD murtabak daging, which are the closest approved analogues sharing the same core composition. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database analogous savoury stuffed wheat-flour pancake category. Confidence is medium rather than low because the analogous foods are compositionally very close and the adaptation is well-documented.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from FAO/INFOODS SEA murtabak (meat, fried) and IMR Malaysia MyFCD murtabak daging entries; no exact martabak telur entry exists in any single approved primary source. 2. Serving size of 200g (6 large pieces) is taken from the vendor description in the input record and is consistent with typical street-food portion sizes for this dish. 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the FAO/INFOODS SEA or IMR MyFCD source entries for this food category. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the FAO/INFOODS SEA or IMR MyFCD source entries for this food category. 5. GI of 57 is an analogous estimate based on savoury stuffed wheat-flour pancake data from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) Diabetes Care; no direct GI measurement for martabak telur was found in the approved literature. 6. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (57 × 46.0g) / 100 = 26.2. 7. Halal status is assumed based on the use of beef or chicken; actual halal certification depends on the specific restaurant and supplier. 8. The input record references Smile Martabak at Funan, Singapore; this is a single-restaurant field survey listing and is not used as a nutritional source. 9. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and water_g are estimated from the FAO/INFOODS SEA and IMR MyFCD analogous murtabak entries and are marked as adapted.
